name: components/media
description: Screenshot and product frames: browser chrome with an address bar, a plain window, or bare shadowed media. Frames make real assets read as intentional.

usage: Requires a real asset: put an or
The chrome row holds ls-media__dots (three <i> dots) and ls-media__address (the product URL); use ls-media--window for native-app framing without the address bar.
The frame fills its grid area, so give it a sized region (a split or dashboard layout) rather than free-flowing content.

composition.itemCountGuidance: 1 media frame per slide; two screenshots compete for legibility.
composition.copyGuidance: Only the address bar carries text; narration belongs next to the frame, not inside it.
useWhen: a product screenshot should read as a browser or app window
a demo recording needs framed context
a split layout pairs UI evidence with narration
avoidWhen: no real screenshot or recording exists
the asset is a diagram or photo rather than product UI
the screenshot's fine detail is the content and cropping would hide it

useCases: Frame a product screenshot in browser chrome with its URL.
Show an app screen in a plain window frame.
Pair UI evidence with narration in a split layout.
